content:
Imagine there are tiny germs that can make you sick. When you take medicine called antibiotics, it’s like sending superheroes to fight the germs and help you feel better. But sometimes, if people don’t take the medicine the right way—like stopping too soon or using it when they don’t need it—the germs get a chance to learn tricks to hide from the superheroes. These germs become stronger and harder to beat. This is called antibiotic resistance.

It’s like if you play a game and your opponent learns your moves, so you can’t win as easily next time. The more the germs practice hiding, the harder it is for medicine to work. That’s why doctors say you should always finish all your medicine and only use it when you really need it. This helps keep the superheroes strong and ready to fight germs whenever you get sick!
